pages.raw_text:
A. D. Charlton, assistant general
passenger agent of the Northern
Pacific, is of the belief that this
will be the banner year for the
northwest. He says more people
of means and prosperous appear
ance are coming west this spring
than before and that nearly all of
them are coming to buy homes or
farms and locate permanently.
There are now at the Northern
Pacific station here, Mr.. Charlton
says, $50 tickets of colonists who
are destined farther west but have
stopped off in Spokane to look
over the country.


The national irrigation congress
at Spokane and the Seattle fair
will mean much to the northwest.
Hundreds of people from the sew
eral irrigated districts in the
United States are coming to Spo-
kane, and nearly all of the visitors
from the east to the A. Y. P. fair
will stop off here. There are 1875
conventions of various kinds to be
held in Spokane, Seattle, Tacoma
and Portland this year, says Mr..
Charlton, all of which will attract
the best kind of people.
